county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Delta County formed 1870 from Hopkins, Lamar. County seat is Cooper. North Texas / Blackland Prairie region with farming and urban growth heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Cooper. Northeast Texas county. Delta County Historical Society. Check Hopkins and Lamar for pre-1870 records. Agricultural heritage. Small-scale farming community.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Delta County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1870; County Clerk in Cooper holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Delta County Deed Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1870 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.

source_profiles.source_name: Delta County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses from 1870. Texas county clerks have maintained marriage records since county formation. State registration from 1966.

source_profiles.source_name: Delta County Probate Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1870. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: Delta County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1870 to 1950 for Delta County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Delta County Land Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Delta County from 1870. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Denton County formed 1846 from Fannin. County seat is Denton. North Texas / Blackland Prairie region with farming and urban growth heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Denton. North Texas county, home to University of North Texas (UNT). UNT Special Collections and the Portal to Texas History (massive digital repository). Denton County Historical Commission. Peter's Colony records at TSLAC. Check Fannin for pre-1846 records. Courthouse-on-the-Square Museum.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Denton County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1846; County Clerk in Denton holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Denton County Deed Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1846 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.

source_profiles.source_name: Denton County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses from 1846. Texas county clerks have maintained marriage records since county formation. State registration from 1966.

source_profiles.source_name: Denton County Probate Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1846. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: Denton County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1850 to 1950 for Denton County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Denton County Enslaved Persons Records -- TSLAC / County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Estate inventories listing enslaved persons, bills of sale, tax rolls listing slaveholders and enslaved persons counts, and Freedmen's Bureau records (NARA). Denton County was part of the Texas plantation economy.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Denton County Land Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Denton County from 1846. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Dickens County formed 1876 from Bexar. County seat is Dickens. Texas Panhandle / South Plains region with ranching and farming heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Dickens. Rolling Plains county organized 1891. Spur Ranch (Espuela Land and Cattle Company) records at Southwest Collection, Texas Tech University. Check Bexar for pre-organization records. Dickens County Historical Commission.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Dickens County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1876; County Clerk in Dickens holds deed books

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1876 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses from 1876. Texas county clerks have maintained marriage records since county formation. State registration from 1966.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1876 to 1950 for Dickens County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.repository: NARA, Fort Worth / Oklahoma Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Pre-settlement and frontier-era records for Comanche and Kiowa in the Dickens County area. Includes Indian Affairs records, military campaign records, and reservation records at NARA Fort Worth. Texas frontier counties experienced significant Native American conflict through the 1870s.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Dimmit County formed 1858 from Bexar, Maverick, Uvalde, Webb. County seat is Carrizo Springs. South Texas / Rio Grande Valley region with Spanish colonial and Mexican land grant heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Carrizo Springs. South Texas brush country. Mexican land grant heritage. Winter Garden area (irrigated farming from 1880s). Check Bexar, Maverick, Uvalde, Webb for pre-1858 records. Strong Mexican-American heritage.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Dimmit County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1858; County Clerk in Carrizo Springs holds deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Donley County formed 1876 from Bexar. County seat is Clarendon. Texas Panhandle / South Plains region with ranching and farming heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Clarendon. Panhandle county organized 1882. Saints' Roost Museum in Clarendon. Clarendon was founded as a temperance colony (1878). JA Ranch territory. Check Bexar for pre-organization records. Clarendon College archives.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Donley County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1876; County Clerk in Clarendon holds deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Duval County formed 1858 from Live Oak, Nueces, Starr. County seat is San Diego. South Texas / Rio Grande Valley region with Spanish colonial and Mexican land grant heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in San Diego. South Texas county. Strong Mexican-American heritage. Duval County Historical Commission. Parr political dynasty records. Check Live Oak, Nueces, Starr for pre-1858 records. Ranching heritage.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Duval County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1858; County Clerk in San Diego holds deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Eastland County formed 1858 from Bosque, Coryell, Travis. County seat is Eastland. Central Texas region with farming, ranching, and diverse immigrant heritage. BURNED COUNTY: 1897 courthouse fire; many records lost.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Eastland. 1897 courthouse fire destroyed many records — check TSLAC for backups. Central-west Texas. Eastland County Museum (home of 'Old Rip' the horned toad). Oil boom era (Ranger oil field, 1917). Check Bosque, Coryell, Travis for pre-1858 records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Eastland County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1858; County Clerk in Eastland holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Eastland County Deed Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1897 to present. Some gaps due to 1897 courthouse fire; many records lost.

source_profiles.source_name: Eastland County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses from 1897. Texas county clerks have maintained marriage records since county formation. State registration from 1966.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1897. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1858 to 1950 for Eastland County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.repository: NARA, Fort Worth / Oklahoma Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Pre-settlement and frontier-era records for various tribes in the Eastland County area. Includes Indian Affairs records, military campaign records, and reservation records at NARA Fort Worth. Texas frontier counties experienced significant Native American conflict through the 1870s.

source_profiles.source_name: Eastland County Oil and Gas Lease Records -- GLO / County Clerk
source_profiles.repository: Texas General Land Office, Austin / County Clerk, Eastland
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Oil and gas lease records, mineral rights transfers, and related land documents from 1917. Texas GLO maintains state mineral records. County Clerk has private mineral lease records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Eastland County from 1858. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Ector County formed 1887 from Tom Green. County seat is Odessa. West Texas / Permian Basin region with ranching, oil, and frontier heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Odessa. Permian Basin oil county organized 1891. Presidential Museum and Leadership Library. Odessa Meteor Crater. Major oil industry records (Permian Basin Petroleum Museum in Midland). Check Tom Green for pre-organization records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Ector County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1887; County Clerk in Odessa holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Ector County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1887 to 1950 for Ector County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Ector County Oil and Gas Lease Records -- GLO / County Clerk
source_profiles.repository: Texas General Land Office, Austin / County Clerk, Odessa
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Oil and gas lease records, mineral rights transfers, and related land documents from 1920. Texas GLO maintains state mineral records. County Clerk has private mineral lease records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Ector County from 1887. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Edwards County formed 1858 from Bexar. County seat is Rocksprings. Texas Hill Country region with German, Czech, and frontier heritage. BURNED COUNTY: 1883 fire; early records destroyed.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Rocksprings. 1883 fire destroyed early records. Hill Country frontier county. Edwards County Historical Commission. Ranching (goats, sheep, cattle). 1927 Rocksprings tornado devastated the town. Check Bexar for pre-1858 records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Edwards County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1858; County Clerk in Rocksprings holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Edwards County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses from 1883. Texas county clerks have maintained marriage records since county formation. State registration from 1966.

source_profiles.source_name: Edwards County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1858 to 1950 for Edwards County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

Includes Indian Affairs records, military campaign records, and reservation records at NARA Fort Worth. Texas frontier counties experienced significant Native American conflict through the 1870s.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Edwards County from 1858. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: El Paso County formed 1850 from Bexar. County seat is El Paso. West Texas / Permian Basin region with ranching, oil, and frontier heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in El Paso. Far western Texas — oldest continuously settled area in Texas (Spanish missions from 1680s). El Paso County Historical Society. Ysleta del Sur Pueblo (Tigua) tribal records. Fort Bliss military records at NARA. Mexican consulate records. Catholic mission registers from 1680s (Ysleta, Socorro, San Elizario). University of Texas at El Paso Library special collections. C.L. Sonnichsen Special Collections.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for El Paso County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1850; County Clerk in El Paso holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: El Paso County Deed Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1850 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.

source_profiles.source_name: El Paso County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses from 1850. Texas county clerks have maintained marriage records since county formation. State registration from 1966.

source_profiles.source_name: El Paso County Probate Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1850. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: El Paso County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1850 to 1950 for El Paso County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Spanish Colonial / Mission Records -- El Paso County Area
source_profiles.repository: Catholic Archives of San Antonio / TSLAC / Bexar Archives at UT-Austin
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Spanish colonial records including mission registers (baptisms, marriages, burials), presidio records, and land grants. El Paso County area was part of the Spanish colonial system. Records at Catholic Archives of San Antonio, TSLAC, and Bexar Archives at UT-Austin.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Colonial-era records vary in survival; many originals at Catholic Archives or in Mexican archives

source_profiles.source_name: Native American Records -- El Paso County Area (Comanche and Kiowa)
source_profiles.repository: NARA, Fort Worth / Oklahoma Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Pre-settlement and frontier-era records for Comanche and Kiowa in the El Paso County area. Includes Indian Affairs records, military campaign records, and reservation records at NARA Fort Worth. Texas frontier counties experienced significant Native American conflict through the 1870s.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for El Paso County from 1850. GLO archives include searchable online databases.
